title: Moving from San Francisco to Austin: Complete Relocation Guide [2026]
description: Compare cost of living, housing, taxes, and quality of life when moving from San Francisco to Austin. Data-driven relocation guide with calculators.
content:
Introduction

San Francisco to Austin is one of the most popular relocation routes in America. Tech workers fleeing sky-high Bay Area costs find Austin offers a comparable tech scene at a fraction of the price.

Cost of Living Comparison

Category San Francisco Austin Savings
Overall Index 269.3 119.3 -56%
Rent (1BR) $3,200/mo $1,650/mo -$1,550/mo
Home Price $1,200,000 $450,000 -63%
Utilities $160/mo $140/mo -13%
Groceries $600/mo $380/mo -37%
Transportation $200/mo $150/mo -25%
Healthcare $500/mo $350/mo -30%

Monthly savings potential: $2,000–$3,000

Salary Calculator

If you make $150,000 in San Francisco, you need approximately $66,000 in Austin to maintain the same standard of living.

Salary Comparison Calculator

You need in to maintain the same standard of living.

Housing Market

San Francisco

  • Median home: $1,200,000
  • Median rent (1BR): $3,200/month
  • Price per sq ft: $1,100

Austin

  • Median home: $450,000
  • Median rent (1BR): $1,650/month
  • Price per sq ft: $285

You can buy a 4-bedroom house in Austin for less than a 1-bedroom condo in San Francisco.

Taxes

Tax Type San Francisco Austin
State Income Tax 9.3% 0%
City Income Tax 0% 0%
Sales Tax 8.625% 8.25%
Property Tax ~0.88% ~1.8%

Annual tax savings on $150K salary: ~$14,000

Climate

San Francisco Austin
Summer High 67°F 96°F
Winter Low 46°F 41°F
Sunny Days 259 228
Humidity Moderate High

Trade-off: Austin summers are hot (100°F+), but you’ll never need a jacket in July.

Culture & Lifestyle

What You’ll Miss from San Francisco

  • World-class dining and Michelin stars
  • Proximity to Napa and Yosemite
  • Dense, walkable neighborhoods
  • Cool summer weather
  • Progressive culture

What You’ll Gain in Austin

  • Live music everywhere
  • BBQ and Tex-Mex
  • No state income tax
  • More space for your money
  • Friendly, laid-back culture

Best Neighborhoods in Austin for SF Transplants

  1. East Austin — Trendy, artsy, most like the Mission District
  2. South Congress — Walkable shops and restaurants, like Hayes Valley
  3. Downtown Austin — High-rise living, like SOMA
  4. Mueller — Planned community, like Mission Bay

Is It Worth It?

Yes — if you can handle the heat and driving.

The financial case is overwhelming: you’ll save $40,000+ annually on housing and taxes. Austin’s tech scene is robust (Apple, Google, Meta, Tesla all have major presence). The main trade-offs are public transit (minimal) and summer heat (extreme).

Disclaimer: All cost of living figures, housing prices, tax estimates, and savings calculations on this page are provided for informational purposes only and do not constitute financial, legal, tax, or real estate advice. Actual costs and savings will vary based on your specific circumstances, lifestyle, income, deductions, and local market conditions. URCity and Northshore Press are not financial advisors, tax professionals, or real estate agents. Always consult qualified professionals before making relocation or financial decisions.

Frequently Asked Questions

Will my salary go down?

Possibly. Tech salaries in Austin are typically 15–25% lower than SF. However, the cost savings more than offset the difference. Many remote workers keep their SF salary.

Is Austin’s tech scene real?

Yes. The “Silicon Hills” nickname is deserved. Major employers include Apple (7,000+ employees), Google, Meta, Tesla, Dell, and thousands of startups.

How’s the food scene?

Excellent, different. Austin is a BBQ and Tex-Mex town, not a fine dining city. But the food is incredible in its own way — Franklin Barbecue, Torchy’s Tacos, and food trucks everywhere.

What’s the biggest culture shock?

The pace and the politics. Austin is liberal by Texas standards but conservative by California standards. Everything moves slower. People are friendlier. There’s no ocean.

Data Sources

  • Cost of living: Council for Community and Economic Research (C2ER)
  • Housing data: Zillow Research
  • Tax data: Tax Foundation
  • Migration data: IRS Statistics of Income

Last updated: June 28, 2026
